seoTitle: How to Update Power BI Desktop to the Latest Version
metaDescription: Keep Power BI Desktop current with monthly feature updates and security patches using the version checker and Microsoft Store automatic updates.
intro: Power BI Desktop ships monthly feature updates and security patches, and staying current keeps your local client compatible with the cloud Power BI Service and Microsoft Fabric. Manually chasing download links isn't necessary if you know where to check your version and how to let updates install automatically.

steps.heading: Check your current version
steps.body: Open the Help or Options menu inside Power BI Desktop to inspect your current build details and confirm whether an update is available.

steps.heading: Use Microsoft Store automatic updates
steps.body: If you installed Power BI Desktop through the Microsoft Store, it typically updates automatically in the background without requiring manual downloads.

steps.heading: Check for manual download options
steps.body: If you installed via a standalone installer rather than the Store, watch for update notifications or check Microsoft's official download page for the latest release.

steps.heading: Validate compatibility after updating
steps.body: After updating, open your existing reports to confirm everything renders correctly and that no visuals were affected by the new release.

faq.question: Does Power BI Desktop update itself automatically?
faq.answer: If installed through the Microsoft Store, updates generally install automatically in the background.

faq.question: How do I know what version I currently have?
faq.answer: Check the advanced options or About menu inside Power BI Desktop, which lists your current build number.

faq.question: Will updating break my existing reports?
faq.answer: Updates are designed to maintain backward compatibility, but it's good practice to validate your reports after any update.

faq.question: Why should I keep Power BI Desktop updated?
faq.answer: Staying current ensures your local design client matches the rendering engine used by the live Power BI Service and Microsoft Fabric, avoiding compatibility issues.
